steps.title: Rewrite both equations in standard form
steps.content:
Simplify the second equation by combining like terms:
$$7r - 5w = 5w + 11$$
$$7r - 10w = 11$$

Rearrange the first equation:
$$6 + 7r = pw$$
$$7r - pw = -6$$

steps.title: Apply elimination and find p
steps.content:
Subtract the second equation from the first to eliminate $r$:
$$(7r - pw) - (7r - 10w) = -6 - 11$$
$$w(10 - p) = -17$$

For this linear equation in $w$ to have no solution, the coefficient of $w$ must be zero while the right side is non-zero. Setting $10 - p = 0$ gives $p = 10$.

When $p = 10$, the equation becomes $0 = -17$, which is impossible. Therefore, $p = 10$.
